hypothesis: If a multimodal model jointly trains CGCNN graph, MatSciBERT-encoded RoboCrystallographer text, and 1D-CNN-encoded simulated XRD via cross-modal latent-space prediction with VIC regularization and contrastive loss, then it will achieve lower MAE on Matbench crystal property tasks (including Band Gap, Shear Modulus, Bulk Modulus, Dielectric, and Perovskite formation energy) and on AFLOW band-gap prediction than state-of-the-art structure-only baselines (CGCNN, CoGN, ALIGNN) while requiring only minimal parameter updates (~0.6%) during finetuning.
